steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "which suggests that" — it must complete a logical inference from the passage. The tension: ~200 quasars formed within the first billion years, yet conditions back then are believed to have been ill-suited to quasar formation. When observed facts contradict current theory, the inference is that the theory is incomplete.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘CTA 102 is thought to have formed less than a billion years after the beginnings of the universe.’: The passage identifies CTA 102 as a quasar but does not state when CTA 102 formed; this choice makes an unsupported claim about that specific quasar rather than drawing the broader inference. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘some aspect of the scientific understanding of quasar formation or the early universe may be incomplete.’: Directly follows from the contradiction: if quasars exist despite supposedly unsuitable conditions, our understanding of those conditions or formation mechanisms must be missing something. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘CTA 102 is likely less massive than quasars that formed more than a billion years after the beginnings of the universe.’: Invents a mass comparison not mentioned or implied anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘quasars that formed in the early universe are likely not as luminous as those that formed later.’: Invents a luminosity comparison with no basis in the passage. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
